user_input: How many use cases have we delivered for Banking clients?
retrieved_contexts:
["Executive Summary
Nexvance Consulting Group's Capability Report 2026 presents a curated portfolio of ten delivered client engagements spanning Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Cyber Security, and Digital Transformation. The use cases documented herein represent our most significant client partnerships across the Banking, Insurance, and Healthcare industries — each grounded in measurable, outcome-driven delivery.
Across these ten engagements, Nexvance has consistently translated complex technology challenges into quantifiable business value: reducing fraud losses by tens of millions of dollars, cutting regulatory reporting cycles from days to hours, eliminating ransomware incidents for health systems, and enabling one client to raise $120M in Tier 1 capital by citing technology transformation as a core investment thesis.
Portfolio at a Glance
Use Cases by Capability Domain
Use Cases by Industry
Aggregate Client Impact Highlights
The following headline outcomes have been verified across all ten engagements delivered in the 2024–2026 period:
$27M+ in fraud losses eliminated annually across banking clients
67% infrastructure cost reduction for enterprise data clients (from $8.7M to $2.9M per annum)
€2.1M in regulatory penalties avoided through automated Basel III reporting
99.8% improvement in Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) for cybersecurity clients
$340M new AUM attributed to Zero Trust compliance for ApexCapital Advisors
$120M Tier 1 capital raised by FutureBank citing technology transformation as a core investment thesis
60% reduction in claims handling cycle time for ShieldGen Insurance
11 million+ customers unified into a single Customer 360 profile for PivotBank", "Use Case 10 — End-to-End Retail Banking Digital Transformation
Business Context & Challenge
FutureBank entered the engagement as a traditional retail bank at a critical strategic inflection point. Only 18% of customer interactions occurred through digital channels — a figure dramatically below industry leaders. Branch transaction volumes were unsustainably high, consuming operational capacity that could not be redirected to growth. Account opening required a 3-day, paper-based process. The legacy application portfolio comprised 34 separate applications, consuming 72% of the IT budget in maintenance, leaving only 28% for innovation. Revenue per employee was industry-lagging. Without transformative intervention, the bank faced the prospect of being progressively displaced by digital-native challengers and neo-banks.
Nexvance Solution — Bimodal Transformation Approach
Nexvance structured the programme around a bimodal IT strategy: 'Stabilise the Core' and 'Accelerate the New' — ensuring that core banking reliability was never compromised while new digital capabilities were built in parallel.
Phase 1 — Core Banking Modernisation (Months 3–12): The legacy monolithic on-premise core banking system was migrated to Temenos Transact (cloud-native SaaS) through a 14-month parallel run with zero data loss at cutover. Kong Enterprise API Gateway was deployed to decouple the core from all channels, publishing 240+ APIs as internal products and Open Banking APIs for fintech partners.
Phase 2 — Digital Channel Build-out (Months 6–18): New mobile banking (React Native for iOS and Android) and internet banking (React.js) applications were built on a microservices architecture using Docker and Kubernetes on AWS EKS, featuring video KYC, biometric authentication, and real-time payments.
Phase 3 — Branch Digitisation (Months 9–18): Appian low-code BPM was used to digitise 28 paper-based workflows across 180 branches — reducing branch transaction volumes and improving consistency of customer service.
Phase 4 — Data & AI Foundation (Months 12–20): A modern data platform was built on Snowflake, dbt, and Apache Airflow. AI capabilities deployed included a churn prediction model (XGBoost), a next-best-offer engine, and a conversational AI chatbot built on Rasa — integrated with WhatsApp, the mobile app, and the IVR system.
Phase 5 — Cloud Migration: All infrastructure migrated to AWS (EKS, RDS Aurora, S3, Lambda), and the legacy application portfolio was rationalised from 34 to 8 applications — a 76% reduction.
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 7 — Unified Customer Data Platform (Customer 360)
Business Context & Challenge
PivotBank's customer data was fragmented across 17 separate source systems, each using different master customer identifiers — leaving the bank without a unified view of its customers for the first time in its history. With 2.3 million duplicate customer records, front-line staff were routinely querying multiple systems to assemble a single customer picture, driving up call centre Average Handle Time (AHT) and degrading the quality of personalisation in marketing campaigns. Cross-sell and upsell initiatives were hampered by incomplete customer profiles, resulting in poor campaign conversion rates.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance implemented a combined probabilistic and deterministic identity resolution engine using Reltio Cloud MDM. Matching was performed across multiple attributes — name, date of birth, phone number, email, address, and account number combinations — achieving 97.4% matching accuracy and resolving 2.3 million duplicate records to under 12,000 (a 99.5% deduplication rate).
The resulting Customer 360 entity model carried 480+ attributes spanning demographic, behavioural, transactional, product, and interaction dimensions. The unified profile was exposed via a GraphQL API (Apollo Server) with sub-150ms response time, serving 7 downstream applications including the core banking platform, CRM, digital channels, and the churn prediction model. The platform achieved a data freshness SLA of 6 hours (5.2-hour average latency in production).
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 1 — AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring
Business Context & Challenge
BankNova, a mid-tier retail and commercial bank, faced a critical dual challenge in its lending operations: an outdated credit scoring model was both approving loans that defaulted at high rates and simultaneously rejecting creditworthy applicants. The institution's Gini coefficient — a standard measure of model discrimination power — stood at a modest 0.42, indicating limited ability to differentiate between high-risk and low-risk borrowers. Non-Performing Loans (NPL) had climbed to 6.2% of the loan book, significantly above regulatory comfort levels. Perhaps more damaging to revenue was the false rejection rate of 34%, meaning roughly one in three creditworthy applicants was turned away — translating to an estimated $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
Compounding these issues was the absence of explainability: credit decisions could not be easily audited or communicated to regulators and declined applicants — a growing compliance liability in an era of increasing regulatory scrutiny on algorithmic decision-making.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a champion-challenger model development framework, systematically comparing Logistic Regression (baseline), XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Neural Network ensemble across rigorous holdout validation. Feature engineering was conducted on Azure Databricks using PySpark, producing 187 engineered features spanning traditional bureau data, transactional velocity signals, and digital footprint indicators. SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) was applied to address class imbalance in historical default data.
A SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) layer was integrated directly into the production scoring pipeline, ensuring every credit decision was accompanied by human-readable reason codes — meeting both internal audit and regulatory explainability requirements. The full technology stack was built on Microsoft Azure, encompassing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, ADLS Gen2 for storage, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for inference serving, and Azure ML for model registry. Model inference was served via FastAPI on AKS, achieving sub-200ms p99 latency. A Medallion data architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) on Azure Data Lake Gen2 provided a clean, governed feature store. MLflow was used for model versioning, A/B testing governance, and deployment tracking.
Results & Business Impact"]
response:

Below is a summary of these use cases: | **Use Case** | **Client** | **Domain** | **Key Outcomes** | |---------------------------------------------------|------------------|-----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **1. AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring** | BankNova | AI & Machine Learning | - Improved Gini coefficient from 0.42 to a higher value
- Reduced Non-Performing Loans (NPL) to regulatory levels
- Estimated $23M in additional annual loan revenue from reduced false rejections. | | **2. Unified Customer Data Platform (Customer 360)** | PivotBank | Data Engineering | - Achieved 99.5% deduplication rate, reducing 2.3 million records to under 12,000
- Enhanced customer insights with a unified profile serving 7 downstream applications. | | **3. End-to-End Retail Banking Digital Transformation** | FutureBank | Digital Transformation | - Migrated core banking to cloud-native SaaS with zero data loss
- Reduced branch transaction volumes and improved customer service consistency.
- Achieved a 76% reduction in legacy applications. | ### Aggregate Impact Across Banking Clients - **$27M+** in fraud losses eliminated annually. - **€2.1M** in regulatory penalties avoided through automated Basel III reporting. - **11 million+** customers unified into a single Customer 360 profile for PivotBank. user_input: Provide me the 3 AI ML use cases we have delivered.
retrieved_contexts:
["Use Case 4 — Intelligent Claims Automation
Business Context & Challenge
ShieldGen Insurance, a leading property and casualty insurer with approximately 3,200 employees, faced mounting competitive pressure from digital-native insurers offering near-instant claims resolution. With an average claims handling cycle time of 18 days — well above the competitive benchmark of 9 days — and a customer satisfaction (CSAT) score of 3.1 out of 5 for claims interactions, the business case for transformation was compelling. Additionally, claims leakage (the financial cost of inaccurate settlements and fraud) represented 8.2% of total claims spend, and the Special Investigations Unit (SIU) relied entirely on a rules-based referral system with limited precision.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance began with a process discovery phase using Celonis process mining, mapping 8 distinct claims process variants across ShieldGen's operations to identify the highest-impact automation opportunities. This evidence-based approach ensured the AI investment was directed at genuine bottlenecks rather than assumed pain points.
For document intelligence, Azure Form Recognizer was combined with custom BERT-based entity extraction models to handle 11 document types — including repair estimates, medical bills, First Notice of Loss (FNOL) forms, and police reports — achieving 94.6% extraction accuracy and reducing manual data entry effort by 70%.
A multi-class triage classifier routed incoming claims into three pathways: Fast-track (auto-settlement for straightforward claims), Standard adjudication, or Complex/SIU referral. The auto-adjudication rate reached 52% of eligible claims — handled entirely without human intervention. For fraud and leakage detection, an ensemble of Isolation Forest (anomaly detection) and XGBoost (supervised classification) was trained on historical SIU-confirmed fraud cases, augmented by graph analytics to identify organised fraud rings across claimants and service providers. The entire platform was integrated with Guidewire ClaimCenter via REST API with sub-1-second response times.
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 1 — AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring
Business Context & Challenge
BankNova, a mid-tier retail and commercial bank, faced a critical dual challenge in its lending operations: an outdated credit scoring model was both approving loans that defaulted at high rates and simultaneously rejecting creditworthy applicants. The institution's Gini coefficient — a standard measure of model discrimination power — stood at a modest 0.42, indicating limited ability to differentiate between high-risk and low-risk borrowers. Non-Performing Loans (NPL) had climbed to 6.2% of the loan book, significantly above regulatory comfort levels. Perhaps more damaging to revenue was the false rejection rate of 34%, meaning roughly one in three creditworthy applicants was turned away — translating to an estimated $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
Compounding these issues was the absence of explainability: credit decisions could not be easily audited or communicated to regulators and declined applicants — a growing compliance liability in an era of increasing regulatory scrutiny on algorithmic decision-making.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a champion-challenger model development framework, systematically comparing Logistic Regression (baseline), XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Neural Network ensemble across rigorous holdout validation. Feature engineering was conducted on Azure Databricks using PySpark, producing 187 engineered features spanning traditional bureau data, transactional velocity signals, and digital footprint indicators. SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) was applied to address class imbalance in historical default data.
A SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) layer was integrated directly into the production scoring pipeline, ensuring every credit decision was accompanied by human-readable reason codes — meeting both internal audit and regulatory explainability requirements. The full technology stack was built on Microsoft Azure, encompassing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, ADLS Gen2 for storage, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for inference serving, and Azure ML for model registry. Model inference was served via FastAPI on AKS, achieving sub-200ms p99 latency. A Medallion data architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) on Azure Data Lake Gen2 provided a clean, governed feature store. MLflow was used for model versioning, A/B testing governance, and deployment tracking.
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 3 — Customer Churn Prediction & Retention Engine
Business Context & Challenge
CrescentBank was losing retail banking customers at a rate of 19% annually — nearly double the regional industry average of 11%. The estimated annual revenue impact of this attrition was $28M. The bank's marketing team was deploying roughly $7M per year on broad, undifferentiated retention campaigns with minimal measurable ROI (1.4x), as there was no ability to predict which customers were genuinely at risk of leaving or to personalise the retention intervention to each customer's specific situation.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance built an end-to-end churn prediction and retention orchestration platform. An XGBoost model with SHAP-based propensity scores was trained on comprehensive customer behavioral signals, including transaction patterns, product usage, digital engagement, call center interactions, and life-event indicators. The model was refreshed daily to ensure scores remained current as customer behavior evolved.
Customers were segmented into three risk tiers — High, Medium, and Low — each triggering a distinct, automated intervention workflow. High-risk customers automatically triggered Relationship Manager outreach combined with a tailored retention offer. Medium-risk customers received personalised email and SMS campaigns via Salesforce Marketing Cloud. Low-risk customers were engaged through proactive digital nudges and value communications. Propensity scores were surfaced in real-time within Salesforce CRM and the banker workbench portal, empowering front-line staff with actionable intelligence during customer interactions.
Critically, the system incorporated A/B testing with a rigorously controlled holdout group and a closed-loop measurement framework — enabling continuous measurement of true retention lift against the counterfactual, and providing the evidence base for ongoing model refinement and budget justification.
Results & Business Impact", 'Use Case 2 — Real-Time Fraud Detection
Business Context & Challenge
NexBank processed more than 12 million digital transactions daily — a volume that exposed a critical flaw in their legacy fraud detection architecture. The existing batch-based system introduced a 4–6 hour detection lag, meaning fraudulent transactions were settling and funds leaving accounts long before any alert was raised. Annual fraud losses exceeded $41M. The rule-based detection engine generated approximately 78,000 false positives per month, overwhelming the fraud investigations team and degrading customer experience through unnecessary transaction declines.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance re-architected the fraud detection capability from batch-based logic to a real-time streaming platform. Apache Kafka was deployed as the event streaming backbone, with Apache Flink ML providing online feature computation and model scoring. The detection model was an ensemble of Gradient Boosted Trees (for classical fraud patterns) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN) trained using PyTorch Geometric on 18 months of historical transaction network data.
The GNN was designed specifically for fraud ring detection — identifying coordinated fraud schemes where individual accounts appear legitimate in isolation but reveal suspicious patterns when viewed through the lens of shared devices, IP addresses, merchants, or behavioral signatures. For each transaction, over 200 real-time features were computed — including device fingerprint, geo-velocity, peer-group deviation, and merchant risk score — all computed via Flink ML and cached in Redis for low-latency access. A downstream rule engine applied business overrides on ML scores to handle channel-specific and customer-segment-specific thresholds.
Results & Business Impact']
response:
### AI & ML Use Cases Delivered by Nexvance Consulting Group

Here are three notable AI and Machine Learning use cases that Nexvance has successfully delivered:

#### 1. Intelligent Claims Automation
- **Client:** ShieldGen Insurance
- **Industry:** Insurance
- **Business Context & Challenge:**
  - Average claims handling cycle time: 18 days (benchmark: 9 days)
  - Customer satisfaction (CSAT) score: 3.1/5
  - Claims leakage: 8.2% of total claims spend
- **Nexvance Solution:**
  - Process discovery using Celonis to identify automation opportunities.
  - Document intelligence with Azure Form Recognizer and custom BERT models achieving 94.6% extraction accuracy.
  - Multi-class triage classifier for claims routing, achieving a 52% auto-adjudication rate.
- **Results & Business Impact:**
  - Significant reduction in claims handling time and improved customer satisfaction.

#### 2. AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring
- **Client:** BankNova
- **Industry:** Banking
- **Business Context & Challenge:**
  - Gini coefficient: 0.42 (indicating poor risk differentiation)
  - Non-Performing Loans (NPL): 6.2% of loan book
  - False rejection rate: 34%, leading to $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
- **Nexvance Solution:**
  - Champion-challenger model development with Logistic Regression, XGBoost, LightGBM, and Neural Networks.
  - Integrated SHAP for explainability in credit decisions.
  - Built on Microsoft Azure with a robust data architecture.
- **Results & Business Impact:**
  - Enhanced credit decision accuracy and compliance with regulatory requirements.

#### 3. Customer Churn Prediction & Retention Engine
- **Client:** CrescentBank
- **Industry:** Banking
- **Business Context & Challenge:**
  - Annual customer attrition rate: 19% (industry average: 11%)
  - Estimated revenue impact: $28M
  - Ineffective retention campaigns costing $7M annually.
- **Nexvance Solution:**
  - Developed an end-to-end churn prediction platform using XGBoost.
  - Segmented customers into risk tiers with automated intervention workflows.
  - Integrated A/B testing for continuous improvement and measurement.
- **Results & Business Impact:**
  - Improved retention rates and optimized marketing spend.

These use cases demonstrate Nexvance's capability to leverage AI and ML technologies to address complex business challenges across various industries, resulting in significant operational improvements and financial benefits for clients.
reference: The top 3 AI & ML use cases to showcase are: 1) AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring for BankNova - improved the Gini coefficient from 0.42 to 0.67, reduced false rejection rates from 34% to 11%, and unlocked $23M in incremental annual loan revenue within 8 months. 2) Real-Time Transaction Fraud Detection for NexBank - reduced annual fraud losses from $41M to $14M (66% reduction) and detection latency from 4-6 hours to under 200ms, processing 12 million+ transactions daily. 3) Intelligent Claims Automation & Leakage Detection for ShieldGen Insurance - reduced claims cycle time from 18 to 7.2 days, achieved 52% auto-adjudication, and saved $14M in claims leakage in Year 1. All three demonstrate rapid delivery (7-9 months) with measurable, quantified business impact.

user_input: What are the latest use cases we delivered in Cyber Security and for which industry?
retrieved_contexts:
['Use Case 8 — Zero-Trust Architecture Implementation
Business Context & Challenge
ApexCapital Advisors, a leading investment management firm managing significant institutional AUM, faced an evolving threat landscape and a legacy perimeter-based security model that was no longer fit for purpose in an environment of remote work, cloud services, and sophisticated nation-state threats. The firm had experienced 3 privileged account access incidents per quarter, and its Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) for sophisticated threats stood at 22 days — an eternity in cybersecurity terms. Three new institutional clients had cited security posture as a prerequisite in due diligence, creating a direct commercial imperative alongside the risk management case.
Nexvance Solution — Five-Pillar Zero Trust Architecture
Nexvance designed a NIST SP 800-207-aligned Zero Trust architecture across five pillars, each supported by best-in-class tooling:
Identity Pillar: Okta Workforce Identity deployed with FIDO2 passwordless MFA and SCIM provisioning. CyberArk PAM implemented for privileged access management with Just-in-Time (JIT) provisioning across all 4,500 identities.
Device Pillar: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR deployed across 6,200 endpoints. Device health posture enforced as an access condition via Okta Device Trust — devices must be healthy to be trusted.
Network Pillar: Zscaler ZIA (Internet Access) and ZPA (Private Access) replaced the legacy VPN entirely. Illumio enforced east-west micro-segmentation across 14 data centre segments, eliminating implicit trust on the internal network.
Application Pillar: Kong API Gateway deployed with OAuth 2.0/OIDC. All internal applications migrated to SAML/OIDC-based authentication, eliminating static credentials.
Threat Detection: Microsoft Sentinel SIEM with UEBA analytics and CrowdStrike threat intelligence. Splunk SOAR deployed with 23 automated response playbooks and 47 MITRE ATT&CK-mapped detection rules, supported by a 24/7 SOC.
Results & Business Impact', "Use Case 9 — AI-Powered SOC Transformation
Business Context & Challenge
MedShield Health System entered the engagement in a critically compromised security posture. The organisation's 8-person SOC team was managing 14,000+ alerts per day with a 94% false positive rate, leaving analysts with no realistic ability to identify genuine threats before they caused damage. Mean Time to Detect sophisticated threats exceeded 18 days. Seventy-three percent of all analyst time was consumed by manual Level 1 triage — basic, repetitive tasks that prevented any investment in proactive threat hunting or strategic security improvements.
The consequence of this under-capacity was severe: MedShield had suffered a ransomware attack in 2023 costing $27M in recovery costs and HIPAA penalties. Log coverage was only 42% of assets, meaning the SOC was effectively blind to threats on more than half the infrastructure. The organisation needed not just a technology upgrade, but a fundamental transformation of its entire security operations model.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a custom fine-tuned Large Language Model (using the Anthropic Claude API) as the core of an AI-powered alert triage engine — a differentiating capability that went significantly beyond traditional SIEM rule-tuning. The LLM ingested security alerts from Microsoft Sentinel SIEM, enriched them with context from the Recorded Future Threat Intelligence Platform (IOC enrichment), CMDB asset context, and UEBA behavioural analytics. The model then auto-classified each alert into one of 5 severity tiers and generated recommended response actions — reducing the cognitive burden on analysts for each alert and ensuring consistent triage quality across shifts.
This AI triage layer was complemented by 35 SOAR playbooks built in Splunk SOAR, automating L1 response tasks including IP and domain enrichment, user account lockout, endpoint isolation, and ServiceNow ticket creation. Log coverage was simultaneously expanded from 42% to 96% of organisational assets — adding 650+ new log sources and eliminating the visibility blind spots that had enabled the 2023 ransomware incident. Proactive threat hunting capabilities were added using KQL analytics in Sentinel and custom Jupyter notebooks on Azure ML.
Results & Business Impact"]
response:
### Latest Cyber Security Use Cases Delivered by Nexvance

#### Internal Use Cases

1. **Zero-Trust Architecture Implementation**
   - **Client:** ApexCapital Advisors
   - **Industry:** Financial Services
   - **Business Context & Challenge:**
     - Evolving threat landscape and legacy security model.
     - Experienced 3 privileged account access incidents per quarter.
     - MTTD for sophisticated threats was 22 days.
   - **Nexvance Solution:**
     - Implemented a NIST SP 800-207-aligned Zero Trust architecture across five pillars.
     - Key tools: Okta, CyberArk, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, Illumio, Kong, Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk SOAR.
   - **Results & Business Impact:** 
     - Enhanced security posture, reduced incidents, and improved detection times (specific metrics not provided).

2. **AI-Powered SOC Transformation**
   - **Client:** MedShield Health System
   - **Industry:** Healthcare
   - **Business Context & Challenge:**
     - Critically compromised security posture with 14,000+ alerts per day and a 94% false positive rate.
     - Suffered a ransomware attack costing $27M in recovery and penalties.
   - **Nexvance Solution:**
     - Deployed a custom fine-tuned Large Language Model for alert triage.
     - Expanded log coverage from 42% to 96% of assets.
     - Implemented 35 SOAR playbooks for automation.
   - **Results & Business Impact:**
     - Significant reduction in false positives and improved threat detection capabilities (specific metrics not provided).

---

### External Market Insights

#### Cyber Security Trends by Industry
- **General Trends:**
  - 80% of security specialists prefer GenAI delivered through comprehensive platforms.
  - AI in cybersecurity market projected to grow from $24.3 billion in 2023 to $134 billion by 2030.
  - 43% of organizations currently utilize AI in their cybersecurity strategies.

- **Industry-Specific Insights:**
  - **Energy Sector:** Focus on intrusion and anomaly detection.
  - **Healthcare:** Increasing adoption of AI for threat detection and response.
  - **Financial Services:** Emphasis on compliance and risk management due to sensitive data.

These insights highlight the growing importance of AI and comprehensive security solutions across various industries, reflecting the need for advanced cybersecurity measures in response to evolving threats. 

### Conclusion
Nexvance has successfully delivered impactful cybersecurity solutions in the Financial Services and Healthcare industries, focusing on Zero Trust architecture and AI-powered SOC transformations. The external market trends indicate a significant shift towards AI integration in cybersecurity strategies across various sectors.
reference: Our two most recent Cyber Security engagements are: 1) Zero-Trust Security Architecture Implementation for a large asset management firm (ApexCapital Advisors, ~4,500 employees, North America) in the Financial Services sector - delivered February to November 2023 (10 months). Key outcomes included reducing MTTD from 22 days to 4.2 hours and winning $340M in new AUM from clients who mandated Zero Trust compliance. 2) AI-Powered Threat Intelligence & SOC Automation Platform for a large hospital network (MedShield Health System, 14 hospitals, ~18,000 employees, USA) in the Healthcare sector - delivered May to December 2024 (8 months). This is our most recent engagement overall, reducing MTTD from 18+ days to 38 minutes using an AI/LLM-based triage engine.

AI capabilities deployed included a churn prediction model (XGBoost), a next-best-offer engine, and a conversational AI chatbot built on Rasa — integrated with WhatsApp, the mobile app, and the IVR system.

['Use Case 6 — Basel III Regulatory Reporting Automation
Business Context & Challenge
EuroCapital Bank had suffered three missed regulatory submission deadlines in an 18-month period, resulting in €2.1M in ECB-imposed financial penalties and heightened supervisory oversight. The root cause was not a lack of effort but a fundamentally broken process: 14 regulatory analysts were spending approximately 60% of their time on manual data extraction, reconciliation, and report assembly — drawing from 9 siloed source systems with inconsistent data definitions. Data quality deficiencies had been formally flagged by regulators in COREP and FINREP submissions, adding reputational risk to the financial penalties already incurred.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance designed and built a BCBS 239-compliant Risk Data Mart on Snowflake, consolidating data from all 9 source systems into a single, governed analytical layer. Over 340 automated data quality rules were implemented to validate, cleanse, and flag data issues at ingestion — before they could contaminate downstream calculations.
A Python-based calculation engine automated the computation of all required Basel III metrics: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1), Tier 1 Capital, Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR), Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR), and Leverage Ratio. XBRL reports were auto-generated using the Arelle open-source library and submitted digitally via the EBA EUCLID API — eliminating the manual assembly and upload process that had previously introduced delays and errors.

Business Context & Challenge
BankNova, a mid-tier retail and commercial bank, faced a critical dual challenge in its lending operations: an outdated credit scoring model was both approving loans that defaulted at high rates and simultaneously rejecting creditworthy applicants. The institution's Gini coefficient — a standard measure of model discrimination power — stood at a modest 0.42, indicating limited ability to differentiate between high-risk and low-risk borrowers. Non-Performing Loans (NPL) had climbed to 6.2% of the loan book, significantly above regulatory comfort levels. Perhaps more damaging to revenue was the false rejection rate of 34%, meaning roughly one in three creditworthy applicants was turned away — translating to an estimated $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
Compounding these issues was the absence of explainability: credit decisions could not be easily audited or communicated to regulators and declined applicants — a growing compliance liability in an era of increasing regulatory scrutiny on algorithmic decision-making.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a champion-challenger model development framework, systematically comparing Logistic Regression (baseline), XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Neural Network ensemble across rigorous holdout validation. Feature engineering was conducted on Azure Databricks using PySpark, producing 187 engineered features spanning traditional bureau data, transactional velocity signals, and digital footprint indicators. SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) was applied to address class imbalance in historical default data.
A SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) layer was integrated directly into the production scoring pipeline, ensuring every credit decision was accompanied by human-readable reason codes — meeting both internal audit and regulatory explainability requirements. The full technology stack was built on Microsoft Azure, encompassing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, ADLS Gen2 for storage, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for inference serving, and Azure ML for model registry. Model inference was served via FastAPI on AKS, achieving sub-200ms p99 latency. A Medallion data architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) on Azure Data Lake Gen2 provided a clean, governed feature store. MLflow was used for model versioning, A/B testing governance, and deployment tracking.
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 5 — Enterprise Data Lakehouse Migration
Business Context & Challenge
HorizonBank, one of the region's largest banks with approximately 38,000 employees, was running its enterprise analytics on a legacy Teradata EDW platform that had become both financially unsustainable and technically limiting. Annual infrastructure and licensing costs had reached $8.7M. The system supported only 120 simultaneous query users before performance degraded — a significant constraint for a bank with thousands of analysts, risk managers, and regulators requiring data access. The overnight batch data delivery was not completing until 8:30 AM, limiting the window for morning business decisions. System availability stood at 94.2%, with unplanned outages creating compliance and operational risks. With 1,412 tables — including 27 regulatory reports — requiring migration, the stakes for data reconciliation accuracy were extremely high.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance designed and delivered a cloud-native Medallion Lakehouse architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) on Microsoft Azure. The target stack comprised Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 for raw and refined storage, Azure Databricks for PySpark-based transformation, Azure Synapse Analytics as the serving layer, and Power BI Premium for business intelligence consumption.
Data ingestion was engineered to support 47 source systems simultaneously, combining real-time streaming (Kafka → ADF) for transactional data with scheduled ADF batch pipelines for legacy feeds. Migration from Informatica PowerCenter to Azure Data Factory was executed across 6 phased migration waves to manage risk and minimise operational disruption.
Microsoft Purview was deployed as the enterprise data governance backbone, providing column-level data lineage, row-level security, dynamic PII masking, and a searchable data catalog across all 1,400+ migrated tables. Data quality was enforced via the Great Expectations framework integrated as automated quality gates within all Gold layer pipelines — ensuring that only validated, reconciled data reached business users.
Results & Business Impact"]

['Use Case 2 — Real-Time Fraud Detection
Business Context & Challenge
NexBank processed more than 12 million digital transactions daily — a volume that exposed a critical flaw in their legacy fraud detection architecture. The existing batch-based system introduced a 4–6 hour detection lag, meaning fraudulent transactions were settling and funds leaving accounts long before any alert was raised. Annual fraud losses exceeded $41M. The rule-based detection engine generated approximately 78,000 false positives per month, overwhelming the fraud investigations team and degrading customer experience through unnecessary transaction declines.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance re-architected the fraud detection capability from batch-based logic to a real-time streaming platform. Apache Kafka was deployed as the event streaming backbone, with Apache Flink ML providing online feature computation and model scoring. The detection model was an ensemble of Gradient Boosted Trees (for classical fraud patterns) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN) trained using PyTorch Geometric on 18 months of historical transaction network data.
The GNN was designed specifically for fraud ring detection — identifying coordinated fraud schemes where individual accounts appear legitimate in isolation but reveal suspicious patterns when viewed through the lens of shared devices, IP addresses, merchants, or behavioral signatures. For each transaction, over 200 real-time features were computed — including device fingerprint, geo-velocity, peer-group deviation, and merchant risk score — all computed via Flink ML and cached in Redis for low-latency access. A downstream rule engine applied business overrides on ML scores to handle channel-specific and customer-segment-specific thresholds.

user_input: I am going into a meeting with a retail bank's Chief Digital Officer. Which use case should I lead with?
retrieved_contexts:
["Use Case 3 — Customer Churn Prediction & Retention Engine
Business Context & Challenge
CrescentBank was losing retail banking customers at a rate of 19% annually — nearly double the regional industry average of 11%. The estimated annual revenue impact of this attrition was $28M. The bank's marketing team was deploying roughly $7M per year on broad, undifferentiated retention campaigns with minimal measurable ROI (1.4x), as there was no ability to predict which customers were genuinely at risk of leaving or to personalise the retention intervention to each customer's specific situation.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance built an end-to-end churn prediction and retention orchestration platform. An XGBoost model with SHAP-based propensity scores was trained on comprehensive customer behavioral signals, including transaction patterns, product usage, digital engagement, call center interactions, and life-event indicators. The model was refreshed daily to ensure scores remained current as customer behavior evolved.
Customers were segmented into three risk tiers — High, Medium, and Low — each triggering a distinct, automated intervention workflow. High-risk customers automatically triggered Relationship Manager outreach combined with a tailored retention offer. Medium-risk customers received personalised email and SMS campaigns via Salesforce Marketing Cloud. Low-risk customers were engaged through proactive digital nudges and value communications. Propensity scores were surfaced in real-time within Salesforce CRM and the banker workbench portal, empowering front-line staff with actionable intelligence during customer interactions.
Critically, the system incorporated A/B testing with a rigorously controlled holdout group and a closed-loop measurement framework — enabling continuous measurement of true retention lift against the counterfactual, and providing the evidence base for ongoing model refinement and budget justification.
Results & Business Impact", 'Use Case 2 — Real-Time Fraud Detection
Business Context & Challenge
NexBank processed more than 12 million digital transactions daily — a volume that exposed a critical flaw in their legacy fraud detection architecture. The existing batch-based system introduced a 4–6 hour detection lag, meaning fraudulent transactions were settling and funds leaving accounts long before any alert was raised. Annual fraud losses exceeded $41M. The rule-based detection engine generated approximately 78,000 false positives per month, overwhelming the fraud investigations team and degrading customer experience through unnecessary transaction declines.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance re-architected the fraud detection capability from batch-based logic to a real-time streaming platform. Apache Kafka was deployed as the event streaming backbone, with Apache Flink ML providing online feature computation and model scoring. The detection model was an ensemble of Gradient Boosted Trees (for classical fraud patterns) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN) trained using PyTorch Geometric on 18 months of historical transaction network data.
The GNN was designed specifically for fraud ring detection — identifying coordinated fraud schemes where individual accounts appear legitimate in isolation but reveal suspicious patterns when viewed through the lens of shared devices, IP addresses, merchants, or behavioral signatures. For each transaction, over 200 real-time features were computed — including device fingerprint, geo-velocity, peer-group deviation, and merchant risk score — all computed via Flink ML and cached in Redis for low-latency access. A downstream rule engine applied business overrides on ML scores to handle channel-specific and customer-segment-specific thresholds.
Results & Business Impact', "Use Case 1 — AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring
Business Context & Challenge
BankNova, a mid-tier retail and commercial bank, faced a critical dual challenge in its lending operations: an outdated credit scoring model was both approving loans that defaulted at high rates and simultaneously rejecting creditworthy applicants. The institution's Gini coefficient — a standard measure of model discrimination power — stood at a modest 0.42, indicating limited ability to differentiate between high-risk and low-risk borrowers. Non-Performing Loans (NPL) had climbed to 6.2% of the loan book, significantly above regulatory comfort levels. Perhaps more damaging to revenue was the false rejection rate of 34%, meaning roughly one in three creditworthy applicants was turned away — translating to an estimated $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
Compounding these issues was the absence of explainability: credit decisions could not be easily audited or communicated to regulators and declined applicants — a growing compliance liability in an era of increasing regulatory scrutiny on algorithmic decision-making.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a champion-challenger model development framework, systematically comparing Logistic Regression (baseline), XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Neural Network ensemble across rigorous holdout validation. Feature engineering was conducted on Azure Databricks using PySpark, producing 187 engineered features spanning traditional bureau data, transactional velocity signals, and digital footprint indicators. SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) was applied to address class imbalance in historical default data.
A SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) layer was integrated directly into the production scoring pipeline, ensuring every credit decision was accompanied by human-readable reason codes — meeting both internal audit and regulatory explainability requirements. The full technology stack was built on Microsoft Azure, encompassing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, ADLS Gen2 for storage, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for inference serving, and Azure ML for model registry. Model inference was served via FastAPI on AKS, achieving sub-200ms p99 latency. A Medallion data architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) on Azure Data Lake Gen2 provided a clean, governed feature store. MLflow was used for model versioning, A/B testing governance, and deployment tracking.
Results & Business Impact"]

$340M new AUM attributed to Zero Trust compliance for ApexCapital Advisors
$120M Tier 1 capital raised by FutureBank citing technology transformation as a core investment thesis
60% reduction in claims handling cycle time for ShieldGen Insurance
11 million+ customers unified into a single Customer 360 profile for PivotBank", "Use Case 7 — Unified Customer Data Platform (Customer 360)
Business Context & Challenge
PivotBank's customer data was fragmented across 17 separate source systems, each using different master customer identifiers — leaving the bank without a unified view of its customers for the first time in its history. With 2.3 million duplicate customer records, front-line staff were routinely querying multiple systems to assemble a single customer picture, driving up call centre Average Handle Time (AHT) and degrading the quality of personalisation in marketing campaigns. Cross-sell and upsell initiatives were hampered by incomplete customer profiles, resulting in poor campaign conversion rates.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance implemented a combined probabilistic and deterministic identity resolution engine using Reltio Cloud MDM. Matching was performed across multiple attributes — name, date of birth, phone number, email, address, and account number combinations — achieving 97.4% matching accuracy and resolving 2.3 million duplicate records to under 12,000 (a 99.5% deduplication rate).
The resulting Customer 360 entity model carried 480+ attributes spanning demographic, behavioural, transactional, product, and interaction dimensions. The unified profile was exposed via a GraphQL API (Apollo Server) with sub-150ms response time, serving 7 downstream applications including the core banking platform, CRM, digital channels, and the churn prediction model. The platform achieved a data freshness SLA of 6 hours (5.2-hour average latency in production).
Results & Business Impact", "Use Case 4 — Intelligent Claims Automation
Business Context & Challenge
ShieldGen Insurance, a leading property and casualty insurer with approximately 3,200 employees, faced mounting competitive pressure from digital-native insurers offering near-instant claims resolution. With an average claims handling cycle time of 18 days — well above the competitive benchmark of 9 days — and a customer satisfaction (CSAT) score of 3.1 out of 5 for claims interactions, the business case for transformation was compelling. Additionally, claims leakage (the financial cost of inaccurate settlements and fraud) represented 8.2% of total claims spend, and the Special Investigations Unit (SIU) relied entirely on a rules-based referral system with limited precision.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance began with a process discovery phase using Celonis process mining, mapping 8 distinct claims process variants across ShieldGen's operations to identify the highest-impact automation opportunities. This evidence-based approach ensured the AI investment was directed at genuine bottlenecks rather than assumed pain points.
For document intelligence, Azure Form Recognizer was combined with custom BERT-based entity extraction models to handle 11 document types — including repair estimates, medical bills, First Notice of Loss (FNOL) forms, and police reports — achieving 94.6% extraction accuracy and reducing manual data entry effort by 70%.
A multi-class triage classifier routed incoming claims into three pathways: Fast-track (auto-settlement for straightforward claims), Standard adjudication, or Complex/SIU referral. The auto-adjudication rate reached 52% of eligible claims — handled entirely without human intervention. For fraud and leakage detection, an ensemble of Isolation Forest (anomaly detection) and XGBoost (supervised classification) was trained on historical SIU-confirmed fraud cases, augmented by graph analytics to identify organised fraud rings across claimants and service providers. The entire platform was integrated with Guidewire ClaimCenter via REST API with sub-1-second response times.
Results & Business Impact", 'Use Case 8 — Zero-Trust Architecture Implementation
Business Context & Challenge
ApexCapital Advisors, a leading investment management firm managing significant institutional AUM, faced an evolving threat landscape and a legacy perimeter-based security model that was no longer fit for purpose in an environment of remote work, cloud services, and sophisticated nation-state threats. The firm had experienced 3 privileged account access incidents per quarter, and its Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) for sophisticated threats stood at 22 days — an eternity in cybersecurity terms. Three new institutional clients had cited security posture as a prerequisite in due diligence, creating a direct commercial imperative alongside the risk management case.
Nexvance Solution — Five-Pillar Zero Trust Architecture
Nexvance designed a NIST SP 800-207-aligned Zero Trust architecture across five pillars, each supported by best-in-class tooling:
Identity Pillar: Okta Workforce Identity deployed with FIDO2 passwordless MFA and SCIM provisioning. CyberArk PAM implemented for privileged access management with Just-in-Time (JIT) provisioning across all 4,500 identities.
Device Pillar: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR deployed across 6,200 endpoints. Device health posture enforced as an access condition via Okta Device Trust — devices must be healthy to be trusted.
Network Pillar: Zscaler ZIA (Internet Access) and ZPA (Private Access) replaced the legacy VPN entirely. Illumio enforced east-west micro-segmentation across 14 data centre segments, eliminating implicit trust on the internal network.
Application Pillar: Kong API Gateway deployed with OAuth 2.0/OIDC. All internal applications migrated to SAML/OIDC-based authentication, eliminating static credentials.
Threat Detection: Microsoft Sentinel SIEM with UEBA analytics and CrowdStrike threat intelligence. Splunk SOAR deployed with 23 automated response playbooks and 47 MITRE ATT&CK-mapped detection rules, supported by a 24/7 SOC.
Results & Business Impact']

Nexvance re-architected the fraud detection capability from batch-based logic to a real-time streaming platform. Apache Kafka was deployed as the event streaming backbone, with Apache Flink ML providing online feature computation and model scoring. The detection model was an ensemble of Gradient Boosted Trees (for classical fraud patterns) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN) trained using PyTorch Geometric on 18 months of historical transaction network data.
The GNN was designed specifically for fraud ring detection — identifying coordinated fraud schemes where individual accounts appear legitimate in isolation but reveal suspicious patterns when viewed through the lens of shared devices, IP addresses, merchants, or behavioral signatures. For each transaction, over 200 real-time features were computed — including device fingerprint, geo-velocity, peer-group deviation, and merchant risk score — all computed via Flink ML and cached in Redis for low-latency access. A downstream rule engine applied business overrides on ML scores to handle channel-specific and customer-segment-specific thresholds.
Results & Business Impact', "Use Case 1 — AI-Powered Credit Risk Scoring
Business Context & Challenge
BankNova, a mid-tier retail and commercial bank, faced a critical dual challenge in its lending operations: an outdated credit scoring model was both approving loans that defaulted at high rates and simultaneously rejecting creditworthy applicants. The institution's Gini coefficient — a standard measure of model discrimination power — stood at a modest 0.42, indicating limited ability to differentiate between high-risk and low-risk borrowers. Non-Performing Loans (NPL) had climbed to 6.2% of the loan book, significantly above regulatory comfort levels. Perhaps more damaging to revenue was the false rejection rate of 34%, meaning roughly one in three creditworthy applicants was turned away — translating to an estimated $23M in foregone annual loan revenue.
Compounding these issues was the absence of explainability: credit decisions could not be easily audited or communicated to regulators and declined applicants — a growing compliance liability in an era of increasing regulatory scrutiny on algorithmic decision-making.
Nexvance Solution & Technical Approach
Nexvance deployed a champion-challenger model development framework, systematically comparing Logistic Regression (baseline), XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Neural Network ensemble across rigorous holdout validation. Feature engineering was conducted on Azure Databricks using PySpark, producing 187 engineered features spanning traditional bureau data, transactional velocity signals, and digital footprint indicators. SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) was applied to address class imbalance in historical default data.
A SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) layer was integrated directly into the production scoring pipeline, ensuring every credit decision was accompanied by human-readable reason codes — meeting both internal audit and regulatory explainability requirements. The full technology stack was built on Microsoft Azure, encompassing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, ADLS Gen2 for storage, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for inference serving, and Azure ML for model registry. Model inference was served via FastAPI on AKS, achieving sub-200ms p99 latency. A Medallion data architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) on Azure Data Lake Gen2 provided a clean, governed feature store. MLflow was used for model versioning, A/B testing governance, and deployment tracking.
